title: Backend event emission + file watcher + snapshot diffing
analysis:
The frontend (bridge.ts + engineStore.ts) already has event listeners for mesh-update, value-update, constraint-update, evaluation-status, diagnostics, and file-changed. The backend currently returns full GuiState snapshots from commands but never emits targeted events. This task adds three capabilities:

1. **Snapshot diffing** (diff.rs): Pure function comparing consecutive GuiState snapshots by key (entity_path for meshes, cell_id for values, node_id for constraints) to produce minimal deltas. Requires adding PartialEq to GUI types.

2. **Event emission**: After set_parameter/update_source/open_file_engine operations in main.rs, diff old vs new state and emit targeted events via Tauri's AppHandle.emit(). Emit evaluation-status events around engine operations.

3. **File watcher** (watcher.rs): Uses notify crate to monitor .ri files. On external change, emits file-changed event. Debounces rapid FS events.

Key architectural constraint: The lib crate (reify_gui) keeps tauri as optional. Diff logic and file watcher are pure Rust (no tauri dependency), fully testable without a Tauri runtime. Event emission (using AppHandle) lives in main.rs behind the gui feature flag. AppState gains a `last_state: Mutex<Option<GuiState>>` field for tracking previous state for diffing.

prerequisites.description: Add notify crate to workspace Cargo.toml (notify = "6") and gui/src-tauri/Cargo.toml dependencies
Add PartialEq derive to MeshData, ValueData, ConstraintData, FileData, GuiState in types.rs so they can be compared for diffing

steps.description: Write test in types_tests.rs: EvaluationStatus serializes to expected JSON format with phase string and optional progress field (matching frontend EvaluationStatus interface)
Add EvaluationStatus struct to types.rs with #[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)] and fields: phase: String, progress: Option<f64> (with skip_serializing_if for progress)
Write test in diff_tests.rs: diff_gui_state with two identical GuiState snapshots returns a StateDelta with empty changed_meshes, changed_values, and changed_constraints vecs

Create diff.rs with StateDelta struct (changed_meshes: Vec<MeshData>, changed_values: Vec<ValueData>, changed_constraints: Vec<ConstraintData>, removed_mesh_paths: Vec<String>, removed_value_ids: Vec<String>, removed_constraint_ids: Vec<String>) and skeleton diff_gui_state that returns empty delta. Register module in lib.rs and tests/mod.rs
Write test: diff_gui_state detects a changed value -- old state has width='80' and new state has width='120', delta should contain exactly that one changed ValueData
Implement value diffing in diff_gui_state: index values by cell_id, compare with PartialEq, include changed values in delta, include new values as changed, track removed value IDs
Write test: diff_gui_state detects a changed constraint -- old has status='Satisfied', new has status='Violated', delta contains that constraint
Implement constraint diffing: index by node_id, compare with PartialEq, include changed/added in delta, track removed IDs
Write test: diff_gui_state detects changed mesh -- old has vertices [0,0,0], new has vertices [1,1,1], delta contains that mesh. Unchanged mesh NOT in delta.
Implement mesh diffing: index by entity_path, compare with PartialEq, include changed/added, track removed paths
Write test: diff_gui_state handles added and removed entities -- new state has a value not in old (appears in changed), old has a value not in new (appears in removed_value_ids)
Verify add/remove logic is correct (should already work from step 6/8/10 implementation). Adjust if needed to pass test.
Write test: StateDelta::full() creates a delta with all meshes/values/constraints from a GuiState (for initial state where there is no previous snapshot)
Implement StateDelta::full(state: &GuiState) that clones all items into changed_* vecs with empty removed_* vecs
Write test: compute_delta with None last_state returns full delta, then stores state. Second call with changed state returns minimal diff delta.
Add last_state: Mutex<Option<GuiState>> to AppState in commands.rs. Implement compute_delta(&last_state, &new_state) -> StateDelta that diffs with last, stores new state, returns delta.
Write test in watcher_tests.rs: Create a temp dir with a .ri file, start FileWatcher, modify file, verify callback is invoked with correct path within timeout
Create watcher.rs with FileWatcher struct using notify crate. Constructor takes a callback Fn(PathBuf). watch(path) method starts watching. Debounce rapid events (100ms). Filter to .ri files only.
Write test: FileWatcher ignores non-.ri file changes -- modify a .txt file in watched dir, verify callback is NOT invoked
Add .ri extension filter in watcher event handler (should already be implemented in step-18, verify it passes)

Wire event emission in main.rs: modify set_parameter, update_source, open_file_engine command wrappers to (1) emit evaluation-status{evaluating}, (2) call impl, (3) compute_delta, (4) emit targeted events for each changed item, (5) emit evaluation-status{idle}. Add emit_delta helper function.

Wire file watcher in main.rs: after creating AppState, if a file is loaded, start FileWatcher on its parent dir. On file change, read file, call update_source_impl, compute_delta, emit events via cloned AppHandle. Store watcher handle to keep it alive.
Wire get_initial_state in main.rs to set last_state (so subsequent commands produce correct diffs) and emit initial evaluation-status{idle}

design_decisions.decision: Diff logic in lib crate, event emission in main.rs binary
design_decisions.rationale: Tauri is an optional dependency (behind gui feature flag). Keeping diff.rs and watcher.rs in the lib ensures they're testable without a Tauri runtime. Only main.rs (which requires gui feature) calls AppHandle.emit().

design_decisions.decision: Add last_state: Mutex<Option<GuiState>> to AppState for diff tracking
design_decisions.rationale: Computing old state before operations would require locking the engine twice (once for old state, once for the operation). Storing the last emitted state is cheaper and simpler. Mutex<Option<>> because initial state is None.

design_decisions.decision: Use notify crate v6 with manual debouncing rather than notify-debouncer-mini
design_decisions.rationale: Fewer dependencies. Manual debouncing (HashMap<PathBuf, Instant>, skip if < 100ms) is simple and sufficient for our use case. The notify crate's own debouncer adds another crate and API surface.

design_decisions.decision: PartialEq on GUI types for diffing comparison
design_decisions.rationale: The simplest approach for comparing snapshots. For MeshData with Vec<f32>, PartialEq compares bit-identical floats, which is correct for our use case -- if the same mesh is regenerated with identical vertices, we should skip it; any change at all should be sent.

design_decisions.decision: StateDelta includes both changed items and removed IDs
design_decisions.rationale: When source is updated, entities may be added or removed. The frontend needs to know about removals (to clean up its store) as well as additions/changes. Removed items are identified by key strings since the full data no longer exists.

design_decisions.decision: FileWatcher takes a generic callback Fn(PathBuf) rather than AppHandle directly
design_decisions.rationale: Keeps watcher.rs in the lib crate with no tauri dependency. The callback is supplied by main.rs with a closure that captures the AppHandle. This also makes the watcher fully testable.

design_decisions.decision: Event names match frontend expectations exactly: mesh-update, value-update, constraint-update, evaluation-status, file-changed
design_decisions.rationale: bridge.ts already has listeners for these exact event names. No frontend changes needed.

reuse.how: Add PartialEq derive for diffing. These are the exact types the diff function compares.
Extend AppState with last_state field. The existing _impl pattern (testable functions taking &Mutex<EngineSession>) is preserved; event emission wraps around it in main.rs.
Same emit pattern used for all new events. AppHandle.emit(event_name, payload) with .ok() to ignore emission errors.
Reuse same pattern for diff tests -- create an EngineSession, load bracket_source, build_gui_state to get test data.
Used in diff and watcher tests to create realistic GuiState snapshots for diffing.
Backend EvaluationStatus struct mirrors the frontend TypeScript interface exactly: { phase: string, progress?: number }
